'''''UFC 298: Volkanovski vs. Topuria''''' was a mixed martial arts event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on February 17, 2024, at the [[Honda Center]] in [[Anaheim]], [[California]], United States.<ref name="Anaheim">{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/11/ufc-298-official-anaheim-feb-17-with-alexander-volkanovski-vs-ilia-topuria-headliner-featherweight-title|title=UFC 298 official for Anaheim on Feb. 17 with Alexander Volkanovski vs. Ilia Topuria headliner|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2023-11-23|access-date=2023-11-23}}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's tenth visit to Anaheim and first since [[UFC 270]] in January 2022.<ref name="Anaheim"/>

A [[UFC Featherweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Alexander Volkanovski]] and [[Ilia Topuria]] headlined the event.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-11-07|title=UFC's Dana White announces upcoming title bouts for UFC 297, UFC 298 and UFC 299|url=https://sports.yahoo.com/ufc-297-298-299-title-fights-dana-white-announce-125011840.html|access-date=2023-11-07|publisher=sports.yahoo.com|author=Rhiannon Walker}}</ref> This bout was originally expected to headline [[UFC 297]] one month prior but was pushed back after Volkanovski was pulled to face [[UFC Lightweight Championship|UFC Lightweight Champion]] [[Islam Makhachev]] at [[UFC 294]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/10/10/23912292/charles-oliveira-out-alexander-volkanovski-now-faces-islam-makhachev-in-ufc-294-main-event|title=Charles Oliveira out; Alexander Volkanovski now faces Islam Makhachev in UFC 294 main event|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2023-10-10|access-date=2023-11-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmaweekly.com/news/alexander-volkanovski-vs-ilia-topuria-set-for-ufc-297|title=Alexander Volkanovski vs. Ilia Topuria set for UFC 297|publisher=mmaweekly.com|author=Jeff Cain|date=2023-10-09|access-date=2023-11-03}}</ref>

A light heavyweight bout between [[Zhang Mingyang]] and Brendson Ribeiro was originally scheduled for [[UFC Fight Night: Song vs. Gutiérrez]].<ref name="Zhang vs. Ribeiro">{{Cite news|title=UFC returns to China for December event & schedules barnburner fight|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/news/ufc-returns-to-china-for-december-event-schedules-barnburner-fight|date=2023-09-16|access-date=2023-09-20|publisher=si.com|author=Mathew Riddle}}</ref> However, the bout was postponed due to the event's change of location and fighters facing visa issues and it was rescheduled to take place this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.itnwwe.com/mma/ufc-shanghai-shifted-to-vegas-ribeiro-vs-zhang-bout-canceled/|title=UFC Shanghai on Dec 9 shifted to Las Vegas, 5 bouts canceled|publisher=itnwwe.com|author=News Desk|date=2023-11-16|access-date=2023-11-20}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.itnwwe.com/mma/brendson-ribeiro-vs-zhang-mingyang-reported-for-ufc-298/|title=Brendson Ribeiro vs Zhang Mingyang reported for UFC 298 PPV|publisher=itnwwe.com|author=News Desk|date=2023-11-21|access-date=2023-11-22}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between former [[UFC Middleweight Championship|UFC Middleweight Champion]] (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: The Smashes]] welterweight winner) [[Robert Whittaker (fighter)|Robert Whittaker]] and former title challenger [[Paulo Costa]] took place at the event.<ref name="Middleweight">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-298-adds-robert-whittaker-vs-paulo-costa-middleweight-contender-clash-anaheim|title=UFC 298 adds Robert Whittaker vs. Paulo Costa in middleweight contender clash|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2023-12-21|access-date=2023-12-21}}</ref> The pair was previously expected to headline [[UFC on ESPN: Whittaker vs. Gastelum]] in April 2021, but Costa withdrew due to illness.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/3/16/22333374/paulo-costa-ill-out-ufc-fight-robert-whittaker-mma|title=Paulo Costa ill, unable to fight Robert Whittaker at April 17 UFC event|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2021-03-16|access-date=2023-12-21}}</ref> They were also expected to meet at [[UFC 284]] in February 2023, but Costa disputed the official announcement by the promotion indicating he had never signed a contract and the fight would not take place.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2022/10/20/23415312/robert-whittaker-vs-paulo-costa-booked-for-ufc-284-in-australia|title=Robert Whittaker vs. Paulo Costa booked for UFC 284 in Australia|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2022-10-20|access-date=2022-10-20}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/12/ufc-284-paulo-costa-vs-robert-whittaker-sell-tickets|title='They should stop being petty': Paulo Costa vs. Robert Whittaker reportedly off the table for UFC 284|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2022-12-19|access-date=2022-12-19}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/12/ufc-284-news-robert-whittaker-out-perth-paulo-costa-upsetting|title=Robert Whittaker confirms he won't remain on UFC 284: 'This is very upsetting'|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2022-12-19|access-date=2022-12-19}}</ref>

A welterweight bout between [[Ian Machado Garry]] and [[Geoff Neal]] took place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ian-garry-vs-geoff-neal-shifted-from-miami-to-ufc-298-in-anaheim|title=Ian Garry vs. Geoff Neal shifted from Miami to UFC 298 in Anaheim|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2023-12-21|access-date=2023-12-21}}</ref> They were previously booked for [[UFC 292]] in August 2023, but Neal withdrew due to undisclosed health reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/08/ufc-292-geoff-neal-out-of-ufc-292-fight-vs-ian-machado-garry-boston-health-issues|title=Geoff Neal out of UFC 292 fight vs. Ian Machado Garry in Boston|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2023-08-09|access-date=2023-12-21}}</ref> They were also scheduled for [[UFC 299]] but were moved to this event for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-299-news-ian-machado-garry-vs-geoff-neal-miami-pay-per-view-welterweight-grudge-match|title=After UFC 296 withdrawal, Ian Machado Garry gets booking against Geoff Neal at UFC 299|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura|date=2023-12-17|access-date=2023-12-21}}</ref>

A welterweight bout between Yusaku Kinoshita and Danny Barlow was scheduled for the event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=News Desk|date=2023-11-15|title=Danny Barlow vs Yusaku Kinoshita reported for UFC 298 PPV|url=https://www.itnwwe.com/mma/danny-barlow-vs-yusaku-kinoshita-reported-for-ufc-298-ppv/|access-date=2024-01-10 |publisher=itnwwe.com}}</ref> However, Kinoshita was removed from event for undisclosed reasons and was replaced by Josh Quinlan.<ref name="Barlow vs. Quinlan">{{Cite web|date=2024-01-08|title=Matchup Roundup: New UFC fights announced in the past week (Jan. 1-7)|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/lists/ufc-news-matchup-fight-announcements-january-1-7-2024|access-date=2024-01-10|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King}}</ref>

A heavyweight bout between [[Tai Tuivasa]] and [[Marcin Tybura]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Mike Heck|date=2023-12-12|title=Tai Tuivasa vs. Marcin Tybura set for UFC 298|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/12/12/23998253/tai-tuivasa-vs-marcin-tybura-set-for-ufc-298|access-date=2024-01-12|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref> However, the bout was moved to headline at [[UFC Fight Night: Tuivasa vs. Tybura]] on March 16.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Steven Marrocco|date=2024-01-12|title=Tai Tuivasa vs. Marcin Tybura moved from UFC 298 to March UFC Fight Night headliner|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/1/12/24036708/tai-tuivasa-vs-marcin-tybura-moved-from-ufc-298-to-march-ufc-fight-night-headliner|access-date=2024-01-12|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref>

A women's strawweight bout between former [[UFC Women's Strawweight Championship]] challenger [[Amanda Lemos]] and [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Joanna vs. Team Cláudia]] strawweight winner [[Tatiana Suarez]] was scheduled for the event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Curtis Calhoun|date=2023-12-12|title=Tatiana Suarez's return booked for UFC 298, opponent revealed|url=https://www.mmanews.com/news/tatiana-suarez-return-ufc-298-opponent-revealed|access-date=2023-12-12|publisher=mmanews.com}}</ref> However, Suarez withdrew from the event due to injury and was replaced by [[Mackenzie Dern]].<ref name="Lemos vs. Dern">{{Cite web|author=Newswire|date=2024-01-16|title=Tatiana Suarez out of UFC 298, Mackenzie Dern now faces Amanda Lemos|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/1/16/24040758/tatiana-suarez-out-of-ufc-298-mackenzie-dern-now-faces-amanda-lemos|access-date=2024-01-17|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref>

[[Road to UFC Season 1#Bantamweight tournament bracket|Road to UFC Season 1]] bantamweight winner [[Rinya Nakamura]] was scheduled to face Brady Hiestand in a bantamweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://asianmma.com/rinya-nakamura-faces-brady-hiestand-at-ufc-298/|title=Rinya Nakamura faces Brady Hiestand at UFC 298|publisher=asianmma.com|author=Staff|date=2023-12-19|access-date=2023-12-19}}</ref> However, Hiestand pulled out due to injury and was replaced by promotional newcomer Carlos Vera, who had previously competed on [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team McGregor vs. Team Chandler|Season 31 of The Ultimate Fighter]] the year prior.<ref name="VeraIn">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/01/ufc-298-fight-card-rinya-nakamura-vs-carlos-vera-brady-hiestand|title=Rinya Nakamura gets new UFC 298 opponent: TUF 31 alum Carlos Vera|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-01-20|access-date=2024-01-20}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between former [[Legacy Fighting Alliance#LFA Middleweight Championship|LFA Middleweight Champion]] [[Anthony Hernandez (fighter)|Anthony Hernandez]] and [[Ikram Aliskerov]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Shaun Al-Shatti|date=2023-12-01|title=Anthony Hernandez vs. Ikram Aliskerov set for UFC 298|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/12/1/23984826/anthony-hernandez-vs-ikram-aliskerov-set-for-ufc-298|access-date=2024-01-21|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref> However, Aliskerov withdrew due to compilations related to an illness and was replaced by [[Roman Kopylov]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Mike Heck|date=2024-01-21|title=With Ikram Aliskerov out, Anthony Hernandez now meets Roman Kopylov at UFC 298|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/1/21/24045692/with-ikram-aliskerov-out-anthony-hernandez-now-meets-roman-kopylov-at-ufc-298|access-date=2024-01-21|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref> The pair was originally expected to take place at [[UFC Fight Night: Grasso vs. Shevchenko 2]] the year prior after Kopylov served as a replacement to Hernandez's original opponent [[Chris Curtis (fighter)|Chris Curtis]], but Hernandez pulled out of the event and the bout never materialized.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2023-08-15|title=Roman Kopylov replaces Chris Curtis, fights Anthony Hernandez at Noche UFC|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/8/15/23832942/roman-kopylov-replaces-chris-curtis-fights-anthony-hernandez-at-noche-ufc|access-date=2024-01-21|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2023-08-23|title=Anthony Hernandez out of UFC Noche, Josh Fremd steps in to fight Roman Kopylov|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/8/23/23842786/anthony-hernandez-out-of-ufc-noche-josh-fremd-steps-in-to-fight-roman-kopylov|access-date=2024-01-21|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref>

Tresean Gore was expected to face A.J. Dobson in a middleweight bout at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/lists/matchup-roundup-new-ufc-fights-announced-in-the-past-week-december-25-31-2024|title=Matchup Roundup: New UFC fights announced in the past week (Dec. 25-Dec. 31)|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-01-01|access-date=2024-02-07}}</ref> However on February 7, Gore pulled out due to a shoulder injury and the bout was scrapped.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/02/ufc-298-news-tresean-gore-out-vs-aj-dobson|title='Gutted' Tresean Gore out of UFC 298 due to shoulder injury|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-02-07|access-date=2024-02-07}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/2/13/24072201/ufc-298-loses-prelim-fight-due-to-injury-card-moves-forward-with-12-bouts|title=UFC 298 loses prelim fight due to injury, card moves forward with 12 bouts|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-02-13|access-date=2024-02-13}}</ref>

A heavyweight bout between [[Justin Tafa]] and [[Marcos Rogério de Lima]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/11/21/23970654/justin-tafa-marcos-rogerio-de-lima-added-ufc-298|title=Justin Tafa vs. Marcos Rogerio de Lima added to UFC 298|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2023-11-23|access-date=2024-02-16}}</ref> However, Tafa withdrew the day before the event due to injury.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/02/ufc-298-anaheim-justin-tafa-vs-marcos-rogerio-de-lima-canceled-weigh-in-day-injury|title=Injury knocks Justin Tafa out of UFC 298 fight with Marcos Rogerio de Lima on weigh-in day|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matt Erickson and Mike Bohn|date=2024-02-16|access-date=2024-02-16}}</ref> He was replaced by his brother [[Junior Tafa]].<ref name="Junior">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/02/ufc-298-news-junior-tafa-vs-marcos-rogerio-de-lima|title=Junior Tafa steps into UFC 298 matchup on one day's notice after brother injured|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King and Mike Bohn|date=2024-02-16|access-date=2024-02-16}}</ref>

During the event's broadcast, former [[List of PRIDE champions#Pride Middleweight championship|PRIDE Middleweight Champion]] and [[PRIDE Final Conflict 2003|2003 PRIDE Middleweight Grand Prix Champion]] (also a former [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship]] challenger) [[Wanderlei Silva]] was announced as the next "pioneer wing" [[UFC Hall of Fame]] inductee during June's International Fight Week festivities in Las Vegas.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/02/ufc-news-wanderlei-silva-joins-2024-ufc-hall-of-fame-class-pride-champion-pioneer|title=Former PRIDE champ Wanderlei Silva joins 2024 UFC Hall of Fame class|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2024-02-17|access-date=2024-02-17}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2024/2/18/24075896/official-bonus-winners-results-ufc-298-ppv-espn-volkanovski-topuria|title=Bonuses! Ilia Topuria cleans up, banks $50K for title fight KO - UFC 298|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2024-02-18|access-date=2024-02-18}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Amanda Lemos vs. Mackenzie Dern'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Ilia Topuria''', '''Anthony Hernandez''', and '''Zhang Mingyang'''

==Reported payout==
The following is the reported payout to the fighters as released by the [[California State Athletic Commission]]. It does not include sponsor money and also does not include the UFC's traditional "fight night" bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/lists/ufc-298-salaries-13-fighters-six-figure-payouts|title=UFC 298 salaries: Alexander Volkanovski leads 13 fighters with six-figure payouts|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Simon Samano|date=2024-02-18|access-date=2024-02-18}}</ref> 
* '''Ilia Topuria:''' $350,000 (no win bonus) def. Alexander Volkanovski: $750,000
* '''Robert Whittaker:''' $400,000 (includes $100,000 win bonus) def. Paulo Costa: $250,000
* '''Ian Machado Garry:''' $110,000 (includes $55,000 win bonus) def. Geoff Neal: $108,000
* '''Merab Dvalishvili:''' $210,000 (includes $105,000 win bonus) def. Henry Cejudo: $150,000
* '''Anthony Hernandez:''' $132,000 (includes $66,000 win bonus) def. Roman Kopylov: $80,000
* '''Amanda Lemos:''' $160,000 (includes $80,000 win bonus) def. Mackenzie Dern: $200,000
* '''Marcos Rogério de Lima:''' $200,000 (includes $100,000 win bonus) def. Junior Tafa: $23,000
* '''Rinya Nakamura:''' $46,000 (includes $23,000 win bonus) def. Carlos Vera: $12,000
* '''Zhang Mingyang:''' $20,000 (includes $10,000 win bonus) def. Brendson Ribeiro: $10,000
* '''Danny Barlow:''' $20,000 (includes $10,000 win bonus) def. Josh Quinlan: $12,000
* '''Oban Elliott:''' $20,000 (includes $10,000 win bonus) def. Val Woodburn: $15,000
* '''Miranda Maverick:''' $150,000 (includes $75,000 win bonus) def. Andrea Lee: $70,000

==Fight night weights==
The following is the official weigh-in weights compared to the fight night weights reported by the [[California State Athletic Commission]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-02-27 |title=UFC 298 fight night weights: Geoff Neal gained over 30 pounds before facing Ian Machado Garry|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/2/27/24084996/ufc-298-fight-night-weights-geoff-neal-gained-over-30-pounds-before-facing-ian-machado-garry|access-date=2025-02-02 |website=MMA Fighting |language=en-US}}</ref>

*'''Alexander Volkanovski''': 145 to 166.2 pounds (21.2 lbs) 15%
*'''Ilia Topuria''': 144.5 to 167.2 pounds (22.7 lbs), 16%
*'''Paulo Costa''': 185.5 to 215.2 pounds (29.7 lbs), 16%
*'''Robert Whittaker''': 185.5 to 207.6 pounds (22.1 lbs), 12%
*'''Ian Machado Garry''': 170.5 to 180.6 pounds (10.1 lbs), 6%
*'''Geoff Neal''': 170.5 to 200.8 pounds (30.3 lbs), 18%
*'''Henry Cejudo''': 135 to 150.6 pounds (15.6 lbs), 12%
*'''Merab Dvalishvili''': 135 to 156.8 pounds (21.8), 16%
*'''Roman Kopylov''': 185 to 194 pounds (9 lbs), 5%
*'''Anthony Hernandez''': 185.5 to 203.6 pounds (18.1 lbs), 10%
*'''Mackenzie Dern''': 116 to 127 pounds (11 lbs), 9%
*'''Amanda Lemos''': 115.5 to 123.8 pounds (8.3 lbs), 7%
*'''Junior Tafa''': 249 to 247 pounds (-2 lbs), -1%
*'''Marcos Rogerio de Lima''': 261.5 to 273.4 pounds (11.9 lbs), 5%
*'''Carlos Vera''': 135.5 to 158.6 pounds (23.1 lbs), 17%
*'''Rinya Nakamura''': 135.5 to 147.2 (11.8 lbs), 9%
*'''Brendson Ribeiro''': 205.5 to 219.6 pounds (14.1 lbs) 7%
*'''Zhang Minyang''': 204.5 to 230.2 pounds (25.7 lbs), 13%
*'''Danny Barlow''': 171 to 197.8 pounds (26.8 lbs), 16%
*'''Josh Quinland''': 169 to 187.8 pounds (18.8), 11%
*'''Val Woodburn''': 169.5 to 184 pounds (14.5 lbs), 9%
*'''Oban Elliott''': 170.5 to 185.2 pounds (14.7 lbs), 9%
*'''Miranda Maverick''': 126 to 135.8 pounds (9.8 lbs), 8%
*'''Andrea Lee''': 125.5 to 136.2 pounds (10.7 lbs), 9%
